title: /kernel
summary_text: `/kernel` could not be activated cleanly in this environment, so I failed closed and recorded it as blocked. The repo窶冱 own contract requires `BLOCKED` when neither `codex + glm + specialist` nor `codex + specialist + specialist` can be established in the current run; that rule is in [.codex/prompts/kernel.md:61](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:61) and enforced by the launch/health path in [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:481](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:481) and [scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh:178](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh:178). I verified local availability and found no repo-usable `codex`, `kglm`, `kgemini`, `kcursor`, or `kcopilot` launcher on `PATH`, and the specialist lane logic expects external binaries or auth-backed adapters rather than repo-local fallbacks. I wrote a truthful bootstrap receipt at [manual_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_fugue-orchestrator-public_kernel-bootstrap_20260402T094500_12603.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/bootstrap-receipts/manual_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_fugue-orchestrator-public_kernel-bootstrap_20260402T094500_12603.json). Health then classified the run as `invalid` with lifecycle `blocked`. The immediate reason surfaced by health was missing repo-local provider evidence, which is expected here because the repo tracks provider usage through its own runtime ledger, not through this Codex session窶冱 internal subagents. To get `/kernel` to go healthy on the next attempt, this machine needs: - a repo-usable `codex` binary or launcher - GLM via `glm` or API-backed credentials - at least one specialist path: `gemini`, `cursor`, or `copilot`/`gh` No repository files were changed.
